market.summary: Markets are broadly positive driven by strong Big Tech AI earnings and S&P 500/Nasdaq at highs, with Asian tech markets hitting fresh records. Geopolitical risk from the Hormuz closure and Trump auto tariff threats temper the upside.
key_drivers: Big Tech earnings beat; AI spending bubble fears dismissed
AMD and SK Hynix surging on AI data center demand
Iran-US Hormuz conflict disrupting global shipping supply chains
Trump auto tariff threats weighing on European markets

Technology.summary: Big Tech earnings validated massive AI capex, AMD surged 74% in April, SK Hynix rallied 12% on data center spending signals, and the data center trade is now broadly mainstream.

Energy.summary: OPEC+ announced a 188,000 bpd output increase bearish for prices, while Hormuz closure continues to disrupt supply chains and keep oil trade choppy.

Financials.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's leadership transition to Greg Abel received positive shareholder reception, and the dropped Fed Chair Powell criminal investigation reduces institutional uncertainty.

Consumer Discretionary.summary: Amazon made a significant strategic move positive for retail sector outlook, while the GameStop-eBay takeover bid injects speculative activity into e-commerce.

Industrials.summary: Three industrials stocks were flagged with warning signs, suggesting selective caution even as the sector has posted strong 6-month gains of 14.6%.

AMD.summary: AMD surged 74% in April and is up 68.4% YTD, ranking as the 21st-best S&P 500 performer, driven by its position as an AI chip leader.

AAPL.summary: Apple is explicitly cited as being in a buy zone as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq trade at all-time highs following strong Big Tech earnings.

AVGO.summary: Broadcom is named alongside Apple as being in a buy zone, benefiting from the strong Big Tech AI earnings cycle.

AMZN.summary: Amazon made a significant strategic move with positive implications for the broader retail sector, reinforcing its competitive position.

META.summary: Cathie Wood's Ark Invest purchased $28.7 million of Meta after a 9% post-earnings decline, signaling institutional buy-the-dip conviction.

BRK.B.summary: New CEO Greg Abel earned broadly positive reviews from Berkshire shareholders at his first annual meeting, ruling out a breakup and emphasizing continuity with Buffett's legacy.
